19.reason: This should be else if, not if, to avoid printing '-' incorrectly when the first condition is true but this one is false

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  This should be else if, not if, to avoid printing '-' incorrectly when the first condition is true but this one is false
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  This should be else if, not if, to avoid falling through to the else branch when the condition is true.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Same issue as above; should be else-if.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Should be else if, not independent if; condition should use > not fabs
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Should be 'else if' to prevent multiple branches from executing and printing extra '-', and condition should be a[i+1] > a[i-1].
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Changed to 'else if' to form a proper conditional chain; otherwise the final 'else' executes incorrectly when this condition is false. Condition also fixed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Using multiple independent 'if' statements instead of 'else if' causes the final 'else' to only apply to the last 'if', leading to incorrect '-' output when earlier conditions match but later ones don't.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Same issue: uses absolute value instead of checking a[i+1] > a[i-1], and doesn't prevent out-of-bound access when i=0. Also, multiple if blocks instead of else-if causes overlapping checks.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Changed to 'else if' to complete the conditional chain; condition also fixed.

14.reason: The condition fabs(...) >= 1 incorrectly allows expansion when the right character is smaller than the left. It should be a[i+1] > a[i-1].

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Condition should be a[i+1] > a[i-1] instead of fabs comparison; also should be part of else-if chain.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Condition should check a[i+1] > a[i-1] instead of fabs; also this block should be part of an else-if chain
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition fabs(...) >= 1 incorrectly allows expansion when the right character is smaller than the left. It should be a[i+1] > a[i-1].
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Condition should check a[i+1] > a[i-1] as per requirements; fabs(...) >= 1 is incorrect and allows reverse order.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Condition uses absolute difference >=1, but problem requires that the character after '-' is greater than the one before. Also, accessing a[i-1] when i=0 leads to undefined behavior (out-of-bound read).]

30.reason: [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (isdigit), not the whole group. So if, e.g., the first 'if' (islower) is true and prints letters, but the last 'if' (isdigit) is false, it still prints '-', causing duplicate or incorrect output like "abcdefghijklmnopqrst-uBCDE-F".]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (isdigit), not the whole group. So if, e.g., the first 'if' (islower) is true and prints letters, but the last 'if' (isdigit) is false, it still prints '-', causing duplicate or incorrect output like "abcdefghijklmnopqrst-uBCDE-F".]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This else only pairs with the last if (isdigit), not the whole group. So if the first or second condition fails but the third also fails, it prints '-', but if any earlier condition matches, it expands AND may still print '-' if later conditions fail. This causes incorrect output like keeping '-' in some cases or duplicating expansions.]
